title: Optional retrieval-grounded explainer (corpus-only) behind the not-advice policy - ships only if eval passes
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task implements the optional retrieval-grounded explainer that answers only from the vetted corpus, behind the not-advice policy layer (fail-closed), and ships only if the eval shows grounded+cited beats blank-slate. It must implement the policy spec (benefits-navigator-policy-001): no-source-no-claim, output screening that rejects un-cited numbers, stateless/trackless operation, and never requesting or retaining personal details.
objective: Implement the corpus-only retrieval-grounded explainer behind the fail-closed not-advice policy layer, gated to ship only if the eval shows grounded+cited beats blank-slate.
acceptanceCriteria: Answers only from the vetted corpus, cites it, carries the not-advice framing + route-to-human pointer, and never invents thresholds/amounts/eligibility rules (no-source-no-claim; output screen rejects un-cited numbers)
Mounted behind the not-advice policy layer (fail-closed; refused intents never reach generation); stateless/trackless; instructed never to request/retain personal details
Ships only if the eval (009 + full eval-018) shows grounded+cited beats blank-slate; otherwise the milestone ships guides + PDFs + data only and the explainer is explicitly deferred
